title: pachycladon wallii
intro: Pachycladon wallii, also known as the New Zealand cabbage tree, is a rare and extinct plant species that belongs to the Brassicaceae family. It is reported to have medicinal properties, particularly in its leaves and seeds, which are said to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The leaves and seeds of Pachycladon wallii are believed to have various health benefits, including the potential to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing. However, due to its extinction, it is no longer available for herbal preparations, but research on its medicinal properties continues.
